summary: Glucose in urine suggests excess sugar beyond kidney reabsorption capacity; this occurs when blood glucose rises above normal thresholds, indicating potential diabetes or impaired glucose metabolism issues. \boxed{\text{"renal threshold for glucose is exceeded"}} This summary captures the essential point about why glucose appears in urine under conditions where it shouldn't normally do so based on physiological limits set by kidneys' handling capabilities.

Answer choice: C
